$comment: RND-9 — the COMMITTED perf ledger: the recorded measurements the ratified bar (perf-bar.json)
is evaluated against. harness/perf-bar.js reads BOTH this ledger and the bar and prints the
head-to-head verdict; scripts/ci-test.sh runs it as the perf-bar gate.
HONESTY (the load-bearing rule of the whole RND track):
• canopy.device  — REAL numbers captured on the live x86_64 emulator by
verified:true. x86_64 emulator → an UPPER BOUND on real-device jank.
the structural host-mutation COUNTS (the no-op-frame=0 hard gate lives here)
• rn             — the RN 0.76.9 reference. RN 0.76.9 is NOT installed in this sandbox, so
these are AUTHORED projections from the byte-identical rn/App.js sibling,
NOT measured here. verified:false. They light up the moment a real rn.json
from a box with the RN toolchain replaces them (perf-bar.js --record-rn).
Re-record canopy.device per CI device/abi class (perf-bar.js --record-device): an x86_64-emulator
ledger row must never be compared to an arm64-device RN row. The gate carries the abi tag.

device.caveat: x86_64 emulator — UPPER BOUND on real-device jank, never a floor. tap-to-paint is the p95 frame-time during the 50-tap burst (a proxy, not an input-latency trace). Re-run on real arm64 for shippable figures.

walker.caveat: device-FREE walker-CPU lane: ns are x86_64 + machine-dependent and measure reconciler cost + host-mutation COUNTS, NOT on-device fps/jank/RSS. The no-op-frame=0 hard gate is proven HERE (structural, not timing).

rn.source: AUTHORED PROJECTION from the byte-identical rn/App.js (FlatList/setState sibling). RN 0.76.9 not installed in this sandbox; replace with a real bench-compare.sh --side rn capture via perf-bar.js --record-rn on a box with the RN toolchain + the SAME device.
rn.caveat: NOT MEASURED HERE. These reference values are deliberately conservative (RN's favour) so a canopy PASS is not manufactured by a soft reference. They exist so the ratified-bar verdict is computable and committed; the verdict is tagged rn.verified=false until a real rn.json lands.
